text: Arriving in this dim, stark hallway, I feel the echo of emptiness and cold efficiency. Light spills harshly onto glass and metal, creating an almost clinical silence. As I move into the second scene, the shift is palpable: color and concept fill the space, with words and screens hinting at innovation and research. The wall’s message about humans and technology intrigues me, but the dark, angled compositions make everything feel slightly off-kilter—exciting, yet disorienting. I’m balanced between curiosity and a subtle unease, pondering the contrasts between sterile reality and mind-stirring creativity.
